front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age <7 years?

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age 10 years (kidnapping)?
back: Abducting a child below 10 years for the sake of moveable property amounts to kidnap (IPC 369 / BNS 97).

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age 7–12 years?
back: Criminal responsibility only if of sufficient maturity to understand and judge the nature and consequences of the act (IPC 83 / BNS 21).

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age <12 years (consent & railway)?
back: Consent of parent/guardian needed before examination; not punishable under the Railway Act.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age <14 years (employment)?
back: Cannot be employed in factory jobs (Factory Act).

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age 14–15 years (employment)?
back: Can be employed in nonhazardous factory jobs in the daytime.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age >15 years (employment)?
back: Can be employed in any job.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: medicolegal importance of age <16 years (kidnapping)?
back: Taking away a boy without consent of parent/guardian amounts to kidnapping.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: list the medicolegal points for age <18 years.
back: Juvenile (boy and girl); taking away a girl without consent of parent/guardian is kidnapping; sexual intercourse with a girl with or without consent is rape (IPC 375 / BNS 63).

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: list the medicolegal points for age ≥18 years.
back: Attainment of majority; can give valid consent to suffer harm not known to cause death/grievous hurt; valid consent by a girl for intercourse; can make a valid will; legal age of marriage for girls.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: what does BNS 63 (IPC 375) cover?

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: what does BNS 64(1) (IPC 376) cover, and its punishment?
back: Punishment for rape — rigorous imprisonment not less than 10 years, which may extend to life.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: what does BNS 65(1) (IPC 376) cover, and its punishment?
back: Rape on a woman under 16 years — rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years, which may extend to life.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: what does BNS 65(2) cover, and its punishment?
back: Rape on a woman under 12 years — rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years, which may extend to life with fine or with death.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: what does BNS 66 (IPC 376A) cover, and its punishment?
back: Rape inflicting injury causing death or a persistent vegetative state — rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years, may extend to life or death.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: what does BNS 67 (IPC 376B) cover, and its punishment?

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology

back: Gang rape on a woman under 18 years — rigorous imprisonment for life and with fine, or with death.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology
front: Marrow: what does BNS 71 (IPC 376E) cover, and its punishment?
back: Repeat offenders — imprisonment for life or with death.

subject: Forensic Medicine & Toxicology

back: Assault or use of criminal force to a woman with intent to outrage her modesty — imprisonment not less than 1 year, up to 5 years, with fine.
